title: quercus × lousae
intro: Quercus × lousae, also known as the hybrid English oak, is a rare deciduous tree resulting from the cross-pollination of the English oak (Quercus robur) and the sessile oak (Quercus petraea). The bark, leaves, and acorns of Quercus × lousae have been used in herbal medicine for centuries, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Quercus × lousae can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
